name: Embalse del Ebro — circular desde Arija
region_intro: Jogging around Merindades offers diverse natural landscapes across the northern Burgos province, characterized by a succession of mountains, rivers, and extensive forests. The region features varied topography, including the significant Ojo Guareña Karst Complex and rolling hills, providing a range of running environments. Numerous rivers like the Nela and Ebro, along with waterfalls and gorges, create scenic backdrops for running routes. This varied terrain supports both gentle paths and more challenging inclines for runners. Best jogging routes around Merindades The most popular jogging route is Ermita y Cuevas de San Bernabé — circular desde Quisicedo, a 5.4 miles (8.7 km) trail that takes about 1 hour to complete. This moderate route explores the area around the Ojo Guareña Karst Complex. Another top favourite among local runners is Cuenca del Cadagua Trail, a moderate 7.5 miles (12.1 km) path. This route follows the Cadagua river basin, offering varied terrain through a scenic valley. Local runners also love the Embalse del Ebro — circular desde Arija, a 3.0 miles (4.9 km) trail leading through reservoir landscapes, often completed in about 30 minutes. Jogging around Merindades is defined by mountains, rivers, forests, and karst complexes, with routes offering options for different ability levels. The routes in Merindades are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from a few reviews. More than 500 runners have used komoot to explore Merindades's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Merindades?
A: Merindades offers a wide variety of running routes, with over 120 trails available on komoot. These range from easy jogs to more challenging runs, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: Are there easy running trails suitable for beginners in Merindades?
A: Yes, Merindades has several easy running options perfect for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1061688961/embalse-del-ebro-circular-desde-arija" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse del Ebro — circular desde Arija&lt;/a&gt; is a gentle 3.0-mile (4.9 km) trail that takes about 30 minutes to complete, offering scenic reservoir landscapes.

Q: Where can I find more challenging running routes in Merindades?
A: For those seeking a challenge, Merindades features over 50 difficult running routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24574350" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Herbosa loop from Espinosa de los Monteros&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 16.2-mile (26.1 km) trail with significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there running routes that feature waterfalls or other natural spectacles?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its stunning waterfalls and gorges. While running, you might encounter natural spectacles like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3970163" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tartalés de los Montes Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the unique natural bridge at Puentedey, carved by the Nela River. The Salceda Waterfall, especially vibrant in spring and autumn, also offers a serene backdrop for runs.

Q: Can I find running trails near the Ojo Guareña Karst Complex?
A: Yes, the Ojo Guareña Karst Complex is a significant natural monument in Merindades. You can explore its surroundings on routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1051337192/ermita-y-cuevas-de-san-bernabe-circular-desde-quisicedo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita y Cuevas de San Bernabé — circular desde Quisicedo&lt;/a&gt;. This area also features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3900706" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Bernabé&lt;/a&gt; and various caves, offering a unique running experience.

Q: Are there running routes along rivers or through scenic valleys?
A: Merindades is characterized by numerous rivers like the Nela and Ebro, and picturesque valleys. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1051328496/cuenca-del-cadagua-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuenca del Cadagua Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, following the Cadagua river basin through varied and scenic terrain. The Mena Valley is also known for its leafy beech forests, ideal for trail running.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect on Merindades' running trails?
A: You can expect a rich variety of natural landscapes, from extensive forests of beech, oak, and hazelnut trees to rolling hills and mountains. The region is considered the greenest area of Burgos, offering a captivating succession of rivers, waterfalls, and gorges, providing diverse and beautiful backdrops for your runs.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in Merindades?
A: Many of the running routes in Merindades are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1051319804/sierra-salvada-y-pico-del-fraile-circular-by-the-zec-monte-santiago" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Salvada y Pico del Fraile — circular by the ZEC Monte Santiago&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate 5.1-mile (8.2 km) loop through varied terrain.

Q: What do other runners enjoy the most about jogging in Merindades?
A: The running routes in Merindades are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Runners often praise the diverse natural landscapes, the quiet trails, and the opportunity to explore unique features like karst complexes and scenic river valleys.

Q: Are there running routes that pass by historical landmarks or points of interest?
A: Yes, Merindades combines natural beauty with historical elements. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20160255" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcázar of the Constables loop from Medina de Pomar&lt;/a&gt; offers a run that includes views of historical architecture. The region's caves also feature ancient hermitages like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3900706" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Bernabé&lt;/a&gt;, adding a cultural dimension to your run.

Q: Can I find long-distance running trails in Merindades?
A: Merindades offers options for longer runs as well. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31446464" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Depósito Iberdrola loop from Trespaderne&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 10.4-mile (16.7 km) route, providing a substantial distance for endurance training amidst the region's varied topography.

Q: Are there running routes in the Mena Valley?
A: Yes, the Mena Valley is a beautiful area for running, known for its leafy beech forests. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10511209" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villasana de Mena loop from Valle de Mena&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 5.8-mile (9.3 km) trail that allows you to experience the valley's natural beauty.
tips: Wonderful place where you can see perfect views of the bridge and one of the entrances to the reservoir.
Beautiful area to go hiking, or simply take a walk since there is no traffic on this dirt road next to these wet areas. When the swamp is very high you can see the bridge almost covered by water.
